The relatives of Jesus were disgusted when they realized there was no chance of worldly advantages from him. Unrighteous people sometimes try to counsel those working in God’s service; but they only advise what seems likely to promote present benefits. The people disagreed about his teachings and miracles, while those who favored him dared not openly express their feelings. Those who consider the preachers of the gospel to be deceivers speak out, while many who favor them fear to receive reproach by expressing regard for them.

Every faithful minister may humbly adopt Christ's words. His doctrine is not his own discovery, but comes from God’s word through the teaching of his Spirit. And amidst the disputes which disturb the world, if any person, of any nation, seeks to do the will of God, he will know whether the doctrine is from God, or whether people speak of themselves. Only those who hate the truth will be given up to errors that will be fatal. Surely it was as agreeable to the design of the Sabbath to restore health to the afflicted as to administer an outward rite. Jesus told them to decide on his conduct according to the spiritual meaning of the Divine law. We must not judge anyone by their appearance, but by their worth and by the gifts and graces of God’s Spirit in them.

Christ proclaimed aloud that they were mistaken in their thoughts about his origin. He was sent by God, who showed himself true to his promises. This declaration, that they did not know God, along with his claim to special knowledge, provoked the listeners; and they sought to seize him, but God can restrain people’s hands even though he does not change their hearts.

The teachings of Jesus convinced many that he was the Messiah; but they lacked courage to acknowledge it. It is a comfort to those who are in the world, but not of it, and therefore are hated by it and weary of it, that they will not be in it always, that they will not remain in it long. Since our times are evil, it is good that they are few. The days of life and grace do not last long; and sinners, when in misery, will welcome the help they now despise. People dispute about such sayings, but events will explain them.

On the last day of the feast of tabernacles, the Jews drew water and poured it out before the Lord. It is supposed that Christ alluded to this. If anyone desires to be truly and forever happy, let him turn to Christ and be guided by him. This thirst means strong desires for spiritual blessings which nothing else can satisfy; so the sanctifying and comforting influences of the Holy Spirit were intended by the waters which Jesus called on them to come to Him and drink. The comfort flows plentifully and constantly as a river; strong as a stream to overcome doubts and fears. There is fullness in Christ, of grace for grace. The Spirit dwelling and working in believers is like a fountain of living water, from which plentiful streams flow, cooling and cleansing like water. We do not expect the miraculous gifts of the Holy Spirit, but we may seek his more common and more valuable influences. These streams have flowed from our glorified Redeemer down to this age and to the remote corners of the earth. May we be eager to make them known to others.

The malice of Christ’s enemies is always against reason, and sometimes the cause for it cannot be explained. No person ever spoke with such wisdom, power, grace, convincing clarity, and sweetness as Christ did. Sadly, many who are temporarily restrained and speak highly of Jesus' word quickly lose their convictions and continue in their sins! People are foolishly swayed by external motives in matters of eternal importance, willing even to be damned for the sake of fashion. As God’s wisdom often chooses things which people despise, so the folly of people commonly despises those whom God has chosen. The Lord brings forward his weak and timid disciples, and sometimes uses them to defeat the plans of his enemies.
